name: Reserve Bank of India
summary: Day 63 Thursday — RBI'S STRATEGIC BATTLE INTENSIFIES: USD/INR ₹94.82 (Apr 29 close, Day 3 of ₹94.50 BREACH; ₹95.00 TRIGGER ₹0.18 AWAY); FOMC HAWKISH-SKEWED DISSENT REMOVES THE DXY-RELIEF VECTOR YESTERDAY'S BRIEF FLAGGED. (1) Powell chose STRUCTURAL framing on energy inflation ('it hasn't even peaked yet'); 4-way FOMC dissent with 3:1 hawkish-vs-dovish ratio (Hammack/Kashkari/Logan voted-with-majority but OPPOSED easing bias = HAWKISH dissent; Miran lone dovish for cut). DXY firmed +0.4% to 98.99 breaks 98.7 ceiling; CME FedWatch 0% Apr cut, 30% by Dec from prior 70% in Mar. The DXY-relief vector that could have provided INR catalyst is STRUCTURALLY REMOVED. (2) Brent closes $118-120 Apr 29 (+6-8%, Day 1 of $115-sustained CONFIRMED; highest since June 2022); Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First makes extended-economic-warfare base case → sustained Brent premium → durable INR pressure. (3) Apr 30 IST 9:15 OPEN faces WORST possible setup: Brent +$5 since previous Indian close, DXY firmer +0.4%, US 10Y +7bps to 4.42% (highest 3M), hawkish FOMC dissent. RBI typically does not pre-signal pre-open. (4) RBI tactical posture: SLOW-THE-DECLINE not defend-fixed-level; selective dollar-sales managing volatility. Indonesian rupiah Apr 29 record low + USD/KRW +1.0% Apr 29 = parallel EM-Asia stress NOT idiosyncratic India. RBI ECL framework clarification settled Bank Nifty FLAT after Tue's -1.5% (regulatory clarification was specific, NOT regime-wide). [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
